title: hydrangea scandens var. lobbii
intro: Hydrangea scandens var. lobbii, also known as mountain hydrangea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hydrangeaceae, native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of mountain hydrangea include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as arthritis, respiratory issues, and skin problems. Mountain hydrangea can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, or used topically as a salve or poultice, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
